requirements.title: Multi-Factor Authentication for EHR System
requirements.description: All users accessing the Electronic Health Records system SHALL use multi-factor authentication before gaining access to patient data.
requirements.verification_method: Penetration testing and manual login test
acceptance_criteria: MFA enabled for all user accounts

requirements.title: Patient Data Encryption at Rest
requirements.description: All patient records stored in the database SHALL be encrypted using AES-256 encryption to protect against unauthorised access.
acceptance_criteria: AES-256 encryption applied to all patient record fields
Encryption keys stored separately from data
Decryption only possible with valid credentials

requirements.title: Role-Based Access Control for Medical Records
requirements.description: Access to patient records SHALL be restricted based on job role. Doctors can view and edit records, nurses can view only, admin staff have no access to medical data.
requirements.verification_method: Access review and user permission audit
acceptance_criteria: RBAC implemented for all system users
Admin staff cannot access patient medical data

requirements.title: Medical Device Network Isolation
requirements.description: All connected medical devices (MRI, ventilators, patient monitors) SHALL be isolated on a dedicated VLAN separate from the general hospital network to prevent lateral movement attacks.
requirements.verification_method: Network audit and VLAN segmentation test
acceptance_criteria: No direct communication between medical VLAN and admin network

requirements.title: Audit Logging for All Patient Data Access
requirements.description: Every access to patient records SHALL be logged with timestamp, user ID, action performed, and IP address. Logs must be retained for a minimum of 12 months.
acceptance_criteria: All patient data access events logged
Logs include timestamp, user ID, action and IP
SIEM alerts on suspicious access patterns
